      chatClient: client,
      instructions: "You are helpful.",
      name: "MyAgent",
      description: "Does things",
      tools: new List<AITool> 
      {
          AIFunctionFactory.Create((string q) => $"Results for {q}", "search", "Search"),
          AIFunctionFactory.Create(SomeMethod, target, "calculate", "Math"),
      },
      loggerFactory: null,
      services: null
  );

Option B: Pass Tools Via ChatOptions in RunAsync
  var options = new ChatClientAgentRunOptions
  {
      ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
      {
          Tools = new List<AITool>
          {
              AIFunctionFactory.Create((string q) => $"Search: {q}", "search", "Search")
          }
      }
  };
  
  var response = await agent.RunAsync(
      "Search for AI",
      session: session,
      options: options,
      cancellationToken: token
  );

Option C: Via ChatClientAgentOptions
  var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
  {
      ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
      {
          Tools = new List<AITool> { /* tools here */ }
      }
  };
  
  var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
      chatClient,
      agentOptions,
      loggerFactory,
      services
  );

================================================================================
5. CHATOPTIONS - FULL CONFIGURATION
================================================================================

ChatOptions (Microsoft.Extensions.AI)
  KEY PROPERTIES FOR AGENT EXECUTION:

  Tools & Behavior:
    - Tools: List<AITool> - Tools available to the model
    - ToolMode: ChatToolMode - How tools are selected (Auto, Required, None, Specific)
    - AllowMultipleToolCalls: bool - Allow multiple tool calls in one response

  Model Parameters:
    - Temperature: double - Randomness (0-2, typical 0.7)
    - TopP: double - Nucleus sampling parameter
    - MaxOutputTokens: int - Max response length
    - Seed: long? - For reproducible results
    - TopLogProbabilities: int - Return log probabilities

  Execution Control:
    - StopSequences: string[] - Stop generation at these sequences
    - ResponseFormat: ChatResponseFormat - JSON mode, etc.

  Example:
    var chatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = toolsList,
        ToolMode = ChatToolMode.Auto,  // Auto-invoke when appropriate
        Temperature = 0.7,
        MaxOutputTokens = 2000,
        AllowMultipleToolCalls = true
    };

================================================================================
6. FUNCTION INVOCATION FLOW
================================================================================

When Model Calls a Tool:

1. Model returns FunctionCallContent in response message with:
   - Name: Tool name
   - Arguments: JSON arguments for the tool

2. FunctionInvokingChatClient (if enabled) automatically:
   - Finds matching AIFunction from Tools list
   - Marshals JSON arguments to function parameters
   - Invokes the function
   - Wraps result in FunctionResultContent
   - Sends back to model for next iteration

3. Stops when:
   - No more function calls requested
   - MaximumIterationsPerRequest reached
   - Unknown tool requested (if TerminateOnUnknownCalls=true)

Example Message Flow:
  User: "Search for AI trends"
  
  → Model sees search tool, returns:
     FunctionCallContent {
         Name = "search",
         Arguments = JsonNode.Parse(@"{""query"": ""AI trends""}")
     }
  
  → Agent invokes search() locally
  
  → Agent sends back:
     FunctionResultContent {
         Result = "AI trends 2025: ..."
     }
  
  → Model sees result, generates final response
  
  → Response: "Based on the search, AI trends include..."

================================================================================
7. SKILL SYSTEM (Agent.Skills)
================================================================================

For Complex Multi-Tool Scenarios:

AgentSkillsProvider:
  - Centralized registration and discovery of tools
  - Organizes tools into "skills"
  - Supports file-based skills (SKILL.md) or in-memory definitions
  
  Skills are organized with:
  - Resources: Static content or delegates returning content
  - Scripts: Executable actions (delegates)
  
  Usage:
    var skillsProvider = new AgentSkillsProviderBuilder()
        .AddInlineSkill(/* skill definition */)
        .Build();
    
    // Use in agent creation...

Classes:
  - AgentInlineSkill: Code-defined skills
  - AgentFileSkill: File-based skills
  - AgentSkillsProvider: Central skills registry
  - AgentSkillsProviderBuilder: Fluent builder for skills

================================================================================
8. CONTEXT PROVIDERS (Optional Enhancement)
================================================================================

AIContextProvider:
  - Base class for enriching context during agent invocation
  - Called at start of invocation to add context
  - Called at end to process results
  
  Common Implementations:
  - ChatHistoryMemoryProvider: Vector-based chat history retrieval
  - TextSearchProvider: External knowledge retrieval (RAG)
  - TodoProvider: In-session todo management
  - FileAccessProvider: File operations
  - FileMemoryProvider: File-based RAG
  - SubAgentsProvider: Sub-task delegation
  
  Usage in AgentOptions:
    agentOptions.AIContextProviders = new List<AIContextProvider>
    {
        new ChatHistoryMemoryProvider(/* options */),
        new TextSearchProvider(/* options */)
    };

================================================================================
9. CHAT HISTORY PERSISTENCE
================================================================================

ChatHistoryProvider:
  - Load chat messages at start of invocation
  - Save new messages after invocation
  
  Interface Methods:
    Task<IEnumerable<ChatMessage>> GetChatHistoryAsync(...)
    Task AddChatHistoryAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages, ...)

Built-in Implementations:
  - InMemoryChatHistoryProvider: Simple in-memory storage
  - ChatHistoryMemoryProvider: Vector-based similarity search

Usage:
    var historyProvider = new InMemoryChatHistoryProvider(
        new InMemoryChatHistoryProviderOptions());
    
    agentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider = historyProvider;

================================================================================
10. BUILDER PATTERN
================================================================================

AIAgentBuilder:
  Fluent API for building agent pipelines with middleware

  Usage:
    var agent = new AIAgentBuilder(innerAgent)
        .Use((agent) => new LoggingAgent(agent))  // Add logging
        .Use((agent) => new OpenTelemetryAgent(agent))  // Add tracing
        .UseAIContextProviders(contextProviders)  // Add context
        .Build();

Extensions:
  - FunctionInvocationDelegatingAgentBuilderExtensions
  - LoggingAgentBuilderExtensions
  - OpenTelemetryAgentBuilderExtensions
  - ToolApprovalAgentBuilderExtensions

================================================================================
11. COMPLETE CODE EXAMPLE
================================================================================

// 1. Create chat client (e.g., from OpenAI)
var chatClient = new OpenAIClient(apiKey).AsChatClient("gpt-4");

// 2. Create tools/functions
var searchTool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    (string query) => $"Results for '{query}'",
    name: "search",
    description: "Search for information"
);

var calculateTool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    (int a, int b) => a + b,
    name: "add",
    description: "Add two numbers"
);

// 3. Create agent options
var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
{
    Id = "research-agent",
    Name = "Research Agent",
    Description = "Helps research topics",
    ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = new List<AITool> { searchTool, calculateTool },
        ToolMode = ChatToolMode.Auto,
        Temperature = 0.7,
        MaxOutputTokens = 2000
    },
    ChatHistoryProvider = new InMemoryChatHistoryProvider(
        new InMemoryChatHistoryProviderOptions())
};

// 4. Create agent
var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
    chatClient: chatClient,
    options: agentOptions,
    loggerFactory: loggerFactory,
    services: null
);

// 5. Create session for multi-turn conversation
var session = await agent.CreateSessionAsync(
    conversationId: "session-123",
    cancellationToken: cancellationToken
);

// 6. Run agent
var response = await agent.RunAsync(
    message: "Search for AI and calculate 5 + 3",
    session: session,
    options: new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(),
    cancellationToken: cancellationToken
);

Console.WriteLine($"Agent Response: {response.Content}");

// 7. Continue multi-turn conversation
var followUp = await agent.RunAsync(
    message: "What about machine learning?",
    session: session,
    options: new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(),
    cancellationToken: cancellationToken
);

================================================================================

Microsoft.Agents.AI
  - ChatClientAgent (Main agent)
  - ChatClientAgentOptions, ChatClientAgentRunOptions
  - AgentSession (For multi-turn)
  - AIAgentBuilder (Middleware pipeline)
  - AIContextProvider (Context enrichment)
  - ChatHistoryProvider (History management)
  - AgentSkillsProvider (Skill registration)
  - Various context providers (TextSearchProvider, TodoProvider, etc.)

Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions
  - AIContextProvider (Base)
  - ChatHistoryProvider (Base)
  - AgentSession, AgentWorkerSession
  - Skill-related abstractions

Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions
  - AITool, AIFunction (Tools/Functions)
  - AIFunctionFactory (Tool creation)
  - AIFunctionDeclaration
  - ChatOptions (Chat configuration)
  - FunctionCallContent, FunctionResultContent
  - Hosted tools (WebSearch, CodeInterpreter, etc.)

Microsoft.Extensions.AI
  - FunctionInvokingChatClient (Auto-invocation)
  - ChatClientBuilder (Builder pattern)
  - Various decorators (Logging, Caching, OpenTelemetry)

================================================================================
13. EXECUTION FLOW DIAGRAM
================================================================================

User Input
  ↓
Agent.RunAsync(message, session, options)
  ↓
AIContextProviders.InvokeAsync (enrich context)
  ↓
ChatHistoryProvider.GetChatHistoryAsync (load history)
  ↓
ChatClient.CompleteAsync with tools list
  ↓
Does model request tool call?
  ├─ YES: FunctionInvokingChatClient catches and invokes
  │   ↓
  │   Find tool in Tools list
  │   ↓
  │   Marshal JSON args to function signature
  │   ↓
  │   Invoke AIFunction locally
  │   ↓
  │   Wrap result in FunctionResultContent
  │   ↓
  │   Loop back to ChatClient with result
  │
  └─ NO: Return final response
      ↓
      FunctionResultContent aggregated
      ↓
      ChatHistoryProvider.AddChatHistoryAsync
      ↓
      AIContextProviders.InvokeAsync (process results)
      ↓
      Return ChatMessage to caller

  - AIFunctionDeclaration
  - ChatOptions (Chat configuration)
  - FunctionCallContent, FunctionResultContent
  - Hosted tools (WebSearch, CodeInterpreter, etc.)

Microsoft.Extensions.AI
  - FunctionInvokingChatClient (Auto-invocation)
  - ChatClientBuilder (Builder pattern)
  - Various decorators (Logging, Caching, OpenTelemetry)

================================================================================
13. EXECUTION FLOW DIAGRAM
================================================================================

User Input
  ↓
Agent.RunAsync(message, session, options)
  ↓
AIContextProviders.InvokeAsync (enrich context)
  ↓
ChatHistoryProvider.GetChatHistoryAsync (load history)
  ↓
ChatClient.CompleteAsync with tools list
  ↓
Does model request tool call?
  ├─ YES: FunctionInvokingChatClient catches and invokes
  │   ↓
  │   Find tool in Tools list
  │   ↓
  │   Marshal JSON args to function signature
  │   ↓
  │   Invoke AIFunction locally
  │   ↓
  │   Wrap result in FunctionResultContent
  │   ↓
  │   Loop back to ChatClient with result
  │
  └─ NO: Return final response
      ↓
      FunctionResultContent aggregated
      ↓
      ChatHistoryProvider.AddChatHistoryAsync
      ↓
      AIContextProviders.InvokeAsync (process results)
      ↓
      Return ChatMessage to caller

================================================================================

data.content:
Build succeeded. Here's what the app does:

**Architecture** — Uses **Microsoft Agent Framework** (`ChatClientAgent`) on top of **Microsoft.Extensions.AI**, following the recommended stack for agentic tool-calling workflows.

**Three tools registered with the agent:**
| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `search_web` | Queries DuckDuckGo Instant Answer API (no API key needed) |
| `add_note` | Saves titled research notes to an in-memory notebook |
| `read_notes` | Reviews all collected notes before summarizing |

**Agent flow:** The system prompt instructs the agent to search 2–3 times with varied queries → save findings as notes → read notes → produce a synthesized summary. The `ChatClientAgent` handles the tool-dispatch loop automatically.

**To run:**
```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
# Optionally: export OPENAI_MODEL="gpt-4o"
cd ResearchAgent && dotnet run
```

Then type a topic like `quantum computing` and the agent will search, take notes, and deliver a summary.

result.content:
================================================================================
MICROSOFT.AGENTS.AI NUGET PACKAGE - COMPLETE API SURFACE GUIDE
================================================================================

PACKAGE VERSIONS:
- Microsoft.Agents.AI: 1.5.0
- Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions: 1.5.0
- Microsoft.Extensions.AI: 10.5.2
- Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions: 10.5.2

================================================================================
1. CORE AGENT CLASSES
================================================================================

ChatClientAgent (Microsoft.Agents.AI)
  SUMMARY: Main agent class that executes conversations using a chat client with tools/skills
  
  CONSTRUCTORS:
    new ChatClientAgent(
        IChatClient chatClient,
        string instructions,
        string name,
        string description,
        IList<AITool> tools,
        ILoggerFactory loggerFactory,
        IServiceProvider services)
    
    new ChatClientAgent(
        IChatClient chatClient,
        ChatClientAgentOptions options,
        ILoggerFactory loggerFactory,
        IServiceProvider services)

ChatClientAgentOptions (Microsoft.Agents.AI)
  SUMMARY: Configuration for agent behavior
  
  PROPERTIES:
    - Id: Agent identifier (string)
    - Name: Agent name (string)
    - Description: Agent description (string)
    - ChatOptions: Base chat options for the agent
    - ChatHistoryProvider: Provider for loading/saving chat history
    - AIContextProviders: List of AIContextProvider for enriching context
    - UseProvidedChatClientAsIs: Don't decorate the chat client
    - ClearOnChatHistoryProviderConflict: Clear history on conflict
    - WarnOnChatHistoryProviderConflict: Log warning on conflict
    - ThrowOnChatHistoryProviderConflict: Throw exception on conflict
    - RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence: Enforce per-call persistence

ChatClientAgentRunOptions (Microsoft.Agents.AI)
  SUMMARY: Runtime options for individual agent invocations
  
  PROPERTIES:
    - ChatOptions: Chat options specific to this run
    - ChatClientFactory: Custom factory to decorate/replace the chat client

================================================================================
2. INVOKING AN AGENT
================================================================================

ChatClientAgent has multiple RunAsync/RunStreamingAsync overloads:

SYNCHRONOUS EXECUTION:
  Task<ChatMessage> RunAsync(string message, ...)
  Task<ChatMessage> RunAsync(ChatMessage message, ...)
  Task<ChatMessage> RunAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages, ...)
  
  // With custom run options
  Task<ChatMessage> RunAsync(
    string message, 
    AgentSession session, 
    ChatClientAgentRunOptions options,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken)

STREAMING EXECUTION:
  async IAsyncEnumerable<StreamingChatCompletionUpdate> RunStreamingAsync(
    string message, ...)
  
  async IAsyncEnumerable<StreamingChatCompletionUpdate> RunStreamingAsync(
    ChatMessage message, ...)

AGENT SESSION:
  // Create a session for multi-turn conversations
  AgentSession session = await agent.CreateSessionCoreAsync(
    conversationId: "conv-123",
    cancellationToken: token);
  
  // Run with session
  ChatMessage response = await agent.RunAsync(
    "What can you do?", 
    session: session,
    options: new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(),
    cancellationToken: token);

================================================================================
3. CREATING TOOLS / FUNCTIONS (Microsoft.Extensions.AI)
================================================================================

AITool / AIFunction Pattern:
  
  AITool is the abstract base class representing a tool
  AIFunction extends AITool with invocable capabilities
  
  Key Types:
    - AITool: Base tool representation (Name, Description, AdditionalProperties)
    - AIFunction: Invocable tool backed by reflection/delegate
    - AIFunctionDeclaration: Tool definition without invocation
    - FunctionCallContent: Message content requesting a function call
    - FunctionResultContent: Message content with function result

CREATING TOOLS with AIFunctionFactory:

  1. From a Delegate:
     var tool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
         (string searchTerm) => 
         {
             return $"Search results for: {searchTerm}";
         },
         name: "search",
         description: "Search for information"
     );

  2. From a Method:
     var method = typeof(SearchService).GetMethod("Search");
     var searchService = new SearchService();
     
     var tool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
         method: method,
         target: searchService,
         name: "search",
         description: "Search for information",
         serializerOptions: new JsonSerializerOptions()
     );

  3. From Instance Methods:
     var tool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
         method: typeof(MyService).GetMethod("DoSomething"),
         target: myServiceInstance,
         options: new AIFunctionFactoryOptions()
     );

  4. Declarative (JSON Schema only, no invocation):
     var declaration = AIFunctionFactory.CreateDeclaration(
         name: "my_tool",
         description: "Tool description",
         jsonSchema: JsonDocument.Parse(@"{
             ""type"": ""object"",
             ""properties"": {
                 ""param1"": { ""type"": ""string"" }
             }
         }").RootElement
     );

AIFunctionFactoryOptions:
  - Name: Override function name
  - Description: Override function description
  - ParameterBindingOptions: Control parameter serialization

================================================================================
4. REGISTERING TOOLS WITH AN AGENT
================================================================================

Option A: Pass Tools During Construction
  var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
      chatClient: client,
      instructions: "You are helpful.",
      name: "MyAgent",
      description: "Does things",
      tools: new List<AITool> 
      {
          AIFunctionFactory.Create((string q) => $"Results for {q}", "search", "Search"),
          AIFunctionFactory.Create(SomeMethod, target, "calculate", "Math"),
      },
      loggerFactory: null,
      services: null
  );

Option B: Pass Tools Via ChatOptions in RunAsync
  var options = new ChatClientAgentRunOptions
  {
      ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
      {
          Tools = new List<AITool>
          {
              AIFunctionFactory.Create((string q) => $"Search: {q}", "search", "Search")
          }
      }
  };
  
  var response = await agent.RunAsync(
      "Search for AI",
      session: session,
      options: options,
      cancellationToken: token
  );

Option C: Via ChatClientAgentOptions
  var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
  {
      ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
      {
          Tools = new List<AITool> { /* tools here */ }
      }
  };
  
  var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
      chatClient,
      agentOptions,
      loggerFactory,
      services
  );

================================================================================
5. CHATOPTIONS - FULL CONFIGURATION
================================================================================

ChatOptions (Microsoft.Extensions.AI)
  KEY PROPERTIES FOR AGENT EXECUTION:

  Tools & Behavior:
    - Tools: List<AITool> - Tools available to the model
    - ToolMode: ChatToolMode - How tools are selected (Auto, Required, None, Specific)
    - AllowMultipleToolCalls: bool - Allow multiple tool calls in one response

  Model Parameters:
    - Temperature: double - Randomness (0-2, typical 0.7)
    - TopP: double - Nucleus sampling parameter
    - MaxOutputTokens: int - Max response length
    - Seed: long? - For reproducible results
    - TopLogProbabilities: int - Return log probabilities

  Execution Control:
    - StopSequences: string[] - Stop generation at these sequences
    - ResponseFormat: ChatResponseFormat - JSON mode, etc.

  Example:
    var chatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = toolsList,
        ToolMode = ChatToolMode.Auto,  // Auto-invoke when appropriate
        Temperature = 0.7,
        MaxOutputTokens = 2000,
        AllowMultipleToolCalls = true
    };

================================================================================
6. FUNCTION INVOCATION FLOW
================================================================================

When Model Calls a Tool:

1. Model returns FunctionCallContent in response message with:
   - Name: Tool name
   - Arguments: JSON arguments for the tool

2. FunctionInvokingChatClient (if enabled) automatically:
   - Finds matching AIFunction from Tools list
   - Marshals JSON arguments to function parameters
   - Invokes the function
   - Wraps result in FunctionResultContent
   - Sends back to model for next iteration

3. Stops when:
   - No more function calls requested
   - MaximumIterationsPerRequest reached
   - Unknown tool requested (if TerminateOnUnknownCalls=true)

Example Message Flow:
  User: "Search for AI trends"
  
  → Model sees search tool, returns:
     FunctionCallContent {
         Name = "search",
         Arguments = JsonNode.Parse(@"{""query"": ""AI trends""}")
     }
  
  → Agent invokes search() locally
  
  → Agent sends back:
     FunctionResultContent {
         Result = "AI trends 2025: ..."
     }
  
  → Model sees result, generates final response
  
  → Response: "Based on the search, AI trends include..."

================================================================================
7. SKILL SYSTEM (Agent.Skills)
================================================================================

For Complex Multi-Tool Scenarios:

AgentSkillsProvider:
  - Centralized registration and discovery of tools
  - Organizes tools into "skills"
  - Supports file-based skills (SKILL.md) or in-memory definitions
  
  Skills are organized with:
  - Resources: Static content or delegates returning content
  - Scripts: Executable actions (delegates)
  
  Usage:
    var skillsProvider = new AgentSkillsProviderBuilder()
        .AddInlineSkill(/* skill definition */)
        .Build();
    
    // Use in agent creation...

Classes:
  - AgentInlineSkill: Code-defined skills
  - AgentFileSkill: File-based skills
  - AgentSkillsProvider: Central skills registry
  - AgentSkillsProviderBuilder: Fluent builder for skills

================================================================================
8. CONTEXT PROVIDERS (Optional Enhancement)
================================================================================

AIContextProvider:
  - Base class for enriching context during agent invocation
  - Called at start of invocation to add context
  - Called at end to process results
  
  Common Implementations:
  - ChatHistoryMemoryProvider: Vector-based chat history retrieval
  - TextSearchProvider: External knowledge retrieval (RAG)
  - TodoProvider: In-session todo management
  - FileAccessProvider: File operations
  - FileMemoryProvider: File-based RAG
  - SubAgentsProvider: Sub-task delegation
  
  Usage in AgentOptions:
    agentOptions.AIContextProviders = new List<AIContextProvider>
    {
        new ChatHistoryMemoryProvider(/* options */),
        new TextSearchProvider(/* options */)
    };

================================================================================
9. CHAT HISTORY PERSISTENCE
================================================================================

ChatHistoryProvider:
  - Load chat messages at start of invocation
  - Save new messages after invocation
  
  Interface Methods:
    Task<IEnumerable<ChatMessage>> GetChatHistoryAsync(...)
    Task AddChatHistoryAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages, ...)

Built-in Implementations:
  - InMemoryChatHistoryProvider: Simple in-memory storage
  - ChatHistoryMemoryProvider: Vector-based similarity search

Usage:
    var historyProvider = new InMemoryChatHistoryProvider(
        new InMemoryChatHistoryProviderOptions());
    
    agentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider = historyProvider;

================================================================================
10. BUILDER PATTERN
================================================================================

AIAgentBuilder:
  Fluent API for building agent pipelines with middleware

  Usage:
    var agent = new AIAgentBuilder(innerAgent)
        .Use((agent) => new LoggingAgent(agent))  // Add logging
        .Use((agent) => new OpenTelemetryAgent(agent))  // Add tracing
        .UseAIContextProviders(contextProviders)  // Add context
        .Build();

Extensions:
  - FunctionInvocationDelegatingAgentBuilderExtensions
  - LoggingAgentBuilderExtensions
  - OpenTelemetryAgentBuilderExtensions
  - ToolApprovalAgentBuilderExtensions

================================================================================
11. COMPLETE CODE EXAMPLE
================================================================================

// 1. Create chat client (e.g., from OpenAI)
var chatClient = new OpenAIClient(apiKey).AsChatClient("gpt-4");

// 2. Create tools/functions
var searchTool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    (string query) => $"Results for '{query}'",
    name: "search",
    description: "Search for information"
);

var calculateTool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    (int a, int b) => a + b,
    name: "add",
    description: "Add two numbers"
);

// 3. Create agent options
var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
{
    Id = "research-agent",
    Name = "Research Agent",
    Description = "Helps research topics",
    ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = new List<AITool> { searchTool, calculateTool },
        ToolMode = ChatToolMode.Auto,
        Temperature = 0.7,
        MaxOutputTokens = 2000
    },
    ChatHistoryProvider = new InMemoryChatHistoryProvider(
        new InMemoryChatHistoryProviderOptions())
};

// 4. Create agent
var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
    chatClient: chatClient,
    options: agentOptions,
    loggerFactory: loggerFactory,
    services: null
);

// 5. Create session for multi-turn conversation
var session = await agent.CreateSessionAsync(
    conversationId: "session-123",
    cancellationToken: cancellationToken
);

// 6. Run agent
var response = await agent.RunAsync(
    message: "Search for AI and calculate 5 + 3",
    session: session,
    options: new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(),
    cancellationToken: cancellationToken
);

Console.WriteLine($"Agent Response: {response.Content}");

// 7. Continue multi-turn conversation
var followUp = await agent.RunAsync(
    message: "What about machine learning?",
    session: session,
    options: new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(),
    cancellationToken: cancellationToken
);

================================================================================
12. KEY NAMESPACE STRUCTURE
================================================================================

Microsoft.Agents.AI
  - ChatClientAgent (Main agent)
  - ChatClientAgentOptions, ChatClientAgentRunOptions
  - AgentSession (For multi-turn)
  - AIAgentBuilder (Middleware pipeline)
  - AIContextProvider (Context enrichment)
  - ChatHistoryProvider (History management)
  - AgentSkillsProvider (Skill registration)
  - Various context providers (TextSearchProvider, TodoProvider, etc.)

Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions
  - AIContextProvider (Base)
  - ChatHistoryProvider (Base)
  - AgentSession, AgentWorkerSession
  - Skill-related abstractions

Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions
  - AITool, AIFunction (Tools/Functions)
  - AIFunctionFactory (Tool creation)
  - AIFunctionDeclaration
  - ChatOptions (Chat configuration)
  - FunctionCallContent, FunctionResultContent
  - Hosted tools (WebSearch, CodeInterpreter, etc.)

Microsoft.Extensions.AI
  - FunctionInvokingChatClient (Auto-invocation)
  - ChatClientBuilder (Builder pattern)
  - Various decorators (Logging, Caching, OpenTelemetry)

================================================================================
13. EXECUTION FLOW DIAGRAM
================================================================================

User Input
  ↓
Agent.RunAsync(message, session, options)
  ↓
AIContextProviders.InvokeAsync (enrich context)
  ↓
ChatHistoryProvider.GetChatHistoryAsync (load history)
  ↓
ChatClient.CompleteAsync with tools list
  ↓
Does model request tool call?
  ├─ YES: FunctionInvokingChatClient catches and invokes
  │   ↓
  │   Find tool in Tools list
  │   ↓
  │   Marshal JSON args to function signature
  │   ↓
  │   Invoke AIFunction locally
  │   ↓
  │   Wrap result in FunctionResultContent
  │   ↓
  │   Loop back to ChatClient with result
  │

      ↓
      FunctionResultContent aggregated
      ↓
      ChatHistoryProvider.AddChatHistoryAsync
      ↓
      AIContextProviders.InvokeAsync (process results)

      Return ChatMessage to caller

result.detailedContent:
================================================================================
MICROSOFT.AGENTS.AI NUGET PACKAGE - COMPLETE API SURFACE GUIDE
================================================================================

PACKAGE VERSIONS:
- Microsoft.Agents.AI: 1.5.0
- Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions: 1.5.0
- Microsoft.Extensions.AI: 10.5.2
- Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions: 10.5.2

================================================================================
1. CORE AGENT CLASSES
================================================================================

ChatClientAgent (Microsoft.Agents.AI)
  SUMMARY: Main agent class that executes conversations using a chat client with tools/skills
  
  CONSTRUCTORS:
    new ChatClientAgent(
        IChatClient chatClient,
        string instructions,
        string name,
        string description,
        IList<AITool> tools,
        ILoggerFactory loggerFactory,
        IServiceProvider services)
    
    new ChatClientAgent(
        IChatClient chatClient,
        ChatClientAgentOptions options,
        ILoggerFactory loggerFactory,
        IServiceProvider services)

ChatClientAgentOptions (Microsoft.Agents.AI)
  SUMMARY: Configuration for agent behavior
  
  PROPERTIES:
    - Id: Agent identifier (string)
    - Name: Agent name (string)
    - Description: Agent description (string)
    - ChatOptions: Base chat options for the agent
    - ChatHistoryProvider: Provider for loading/saving chat history
    - AIContextProviders: List of AIContextProvider for enriching context
    - UseProvidedChatClientAsIs: Don't decorate the chat client
    - ClearOnChatHistoryProviderConflict: Clear history on conflict
    - WarnOnChatHistoryProviderConflict: Log warning on conflict
    - ThrowOnChatHistoryProviderConflict: Throw exception on conflict
    - RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence: Enforce per-call persistence

ChatClientAgentRunOptions (Microsoft.Agents.AI)
  SUMMARY: Runtime options for individual agent invocations
  
  PROPERTIES:
    - ChatOptions: Chat options specific to this run
    - ChatClientFactory: Custom factory to decorate/replace the chat client

================================================================================
2. INVOKING AN AGENT
================================================================================

ChatClientAgent has multiple RunAsync/RunStreamingAsync overloads:

SYNCHRONOUS EXECUTION:
  Task<ChatMessage> RunAsync(string message, ...)
  Task<ChatMessage> RunAsync(ChatMessage message, ...)
  Task<ChatMessage> RunAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages, ...)
  
  // With custom run options
  Task<ChatMessage> RunAsync(
    string message, 
    AgentSession session, 
    ChatClientAgentRunOptions options,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken)

STREAMING EXECUTION:
  async IAsyncEnumerable<StreamingChatCompletionUpdate> RunStreamingAsync(
    string message, ...)
  
  async IAsyncEnumerable<StreamingChatCompletionUpdate> RunStreamingAsync(
    ChatMessage message, ...)

AGENT SESSION:
  // Create a session for multi-turn conversations
  AgentSession session = await agent.CreateSessionCoreAsync(
    conversationId: "conv-123",
    cancellationToken: token);
  
  // Run with session
  ChatMessage response = await agent.RunAsync(
    "What can you do?", 
    session: session,
    options: new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(),
    cancellationToken: token);

================================================================================
3. CREATING TOOLS / FUNCTIONS (Microsoft.Extensions.AI)
================================================================================

AITool / AIFunction Pattern:
  
  AITool is the abstract base class representing a tool
  AIFunction extends AITool with invocable capabilities
  
  Key Types:
    - AITool: Base tool representation (Name, Description, AdditionalProperties)
    - AIFunction: Invocable tool backed by reflection/delegate
    - AIFunctionDeclaration: Tool definition without invocation
    - FunctionCallContent: Message content requesting a function call
    - FunctionResultContent: Message content with function result

CREATING TOOLS with AIFunctionFactory:

  1. From a Delegate:
     var tool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
         (string searchTerm) => 
         {
             return $"Search results for: {searchTerm}";
         },
         name: "search",
         description: "Search for information"
     );

  2. From a Method:
     var method = typeof(SearchService).GetMethod("Search");
     var searchService = new SearchService();
     
     var tool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
         method: method,
         target: searchService,
         name: "search",
         description: "Search for information",
         serializerOptions: new JsonSerializerOptions()
     );

  3. From Instance Methods:
     var tool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
         method: typeof(MyService).GetMethod("DoSomething"),
         target: myServiceInstance,
         options: new AIFunctionFactoryOptions()
     );

  4. Declarative (JSON Schema only, no invocation):
     var declaration = AIFunctionFactory.CreateDeclaration(
         name: "my_tool",
         description: "Tool description",
         jsonSchema: JsonDocument.Parse(@"{
             ""type"": ""object"",
             ""properties"": {
                 ""param1"": { ""type"": ""string"" }
             }
         }").RootElement
     );

AIFunctionFactoryOptions:
  - Name: Override function name
  - Description: Override function description
  - ParameterBindingOptions: Control parameter serialization

================================================================================
4. REGISTERING TOOLS WITH AN AGENT
================================================================================

Option A: Pass Tools During Construction
  var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
      chatClient: client,
      instructions: "You are helpful.",
      name: "MyAgent",
      description: "Does things",
      tools: new List<AITool> 
      {
          AIFunctionFactory.Create((string q) => $"Results for {q}", "search", "Search"),
          AIFunctionFactory.Create(SomeMethod, target, "calculate", "Math"),
      },
      loggerFactory: null,
      services: null
  );

Option B: Pass Tools Via ChatOptions in RunAsync
  var options = new ChatClientAgentRunOptions
  {
      ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
      {
          Tools = new List<AITool>
          {
              AIFunctionFactory.Create((string q) => $"Search: {q}", "search", "Search")
          }
      }
  };
  
  var response = await agent.RunAsync(
      "Search for AI",
      session: session,
      options: options,
      cancellationToken: token
  );

Option C: Via ChatClientAgentOptions
  var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
  {
      ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
      {
          Tools = new List<AITool> { /* tools here */ }
      }
  };
  
  var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
      chatClient,
      agentOptions,
      loggerFactory,
      services
  );

================================================================================
5. CHATOPTIONS - FULL CONFIGURATION
================================================================================

ChatOptions (Microsoft.Extensions.AI)
  KEY PROPERTIES FOR AGENT EXECUTION:

  Tools & Behavior:
    - Tools: List<AITool> - Tools available to the model
    - ToolMode: ChatToolMode - How tools are selected (Auto, Required, None, Specific)
    - AllowMultipleToolCalls: bool - Allow multiple tool calls in one response

  Model Parameters:
    - Temperature: double - Randomness (0-2, typical 0.7)
    - TopP: double - Nucleus sampling parameter
    - MaxOutputTokens: int - Max response length
    - Seed: long? - For reproducible results
    - TopLogProbabilities: int - Return log probabilities

  Execution Control:
    - StopSequences: string[] - Stop generation at these sequences
    - ResponseFormat: ChatResponseFormat - JSON mode, etc.

  Example:
    var chatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = toolsList,
        ToolMode = ChatToolMode.Auto,  // Auto-invoke when appropriate
        Temperature = 0.7,
        MaxOutputTokens = 2000,
        AllowMultipleToolCalls = true
    };

================================================================================
6. FUNCTION INVOCATION FLOW
================================================================================

When Model Calls a Tool:

1. Model returns FunctionCallContent in response message with:
   - Name: Tool name
   - Arguments: JSON arguments for the tool

2. FunctionInvokingChatClient (if enabled) automatically:
   - Finds matching AIFunction from Tools list
   - Marshals JSON arguments to function parameters
   - Invokes the function
   - Wraps result in FunctionResultContent
   - Sends back to model for next iteration

3. Stops when:
   - No more function calls requested
   - MaximumIterationsPerRequest reached
   - Unknown tool requested (if TerminateOnUnknownCalls=true)

Example Message Flow:
  User: "Search for AI trends"
  
  → Model sees search tool, returns:
     FunctionCallContent {
         Name = "search",
         Arguments = JsonNode.Parse(@"{""query"": ""AI trends""}")
     }
  
  → Agent invokes search() locally
  
  → Agent sends back:
     FunctionResultContent {
         Result = "AI trends 2025: ..."
     }
  
  → Model sees result, generates final response
  
  → Response: "Based on the search, AI trends include..."

================================================================================
7. SKILL SYSTEM (Agent.Skills)
================================================================================

For Complex Multi-Tool Scenarios:

AgentSkillsProvider:
  - Centralized registration and discovery of tools
  - Organizes tools into "skills"
  - Supports file-based skills (SKILL.md) or in-memory definitions
  
  Skills are organized with:
  - Resources: Static content or delegates returning content
  - Scripts: Executable actions (delegates)
  
  Usage:
    var skillsProvider = new AgentSkillsProviderBuilder()
        .AddInlineSkill(/* skill definition */)
        .Build();
    
    // Use in agent creation...

Classes:
  - AgentInlineSkill: Code-defined skills
  - AgentFileSkill: File-based skills
  - AgentSkillsProvider: Central skills registry
  - AgentSkillsProviderBuilder: Fluent builder for skills

================================================================================
8. CONTEXT PROVIDERS (Optional Enhancement)
================================================================================

AIContextProvider:
  - Base class for enriching context during agent invocation
  - Called at start of invocation to add context
  - Called at end to process results
  
  Common Implementations:
  - ChatHistoryMemoryProvider: Vector-based chat history retrieval
  - TextSearchProvider: External knowledge retrieval (RAG)
  - TodoProvider: In-session todo management
  - FileAccessProvider: File operations
  - FileMemoryProvider: File-based RAG
  - SubAgentsProvider: Sub-task delegation
  
  Usage in AgentOptions:
    agentOptions.AIContextProviders = new List<AIContextProvider>
    {
        new ChatHistoryMemoryProvider(/* options */),
        new TextSearchProvider(/* options */)
    };

================================================================================
9. CHAT HISTORY PERSISTENCE
================================================================================

ChatHistoryProvider:
  - Load chat messages at start of invocation
  - Save new messages after invocation
  
  Interface Methods:
    Task<IEnumerable<ChatMessage>> GetChatHistoryAsync(...)
    Task AddChatHistoryAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages, ...)

Built-in Implementations:
  - InMemoryChatHistoryProvider: Simple in-memory storage
  - ChatHistoryMemoryProvider: Vector-based similarity search

Usage:
    var historyProvider = new InMemoryChatHistoryProvider(
        new InMemoryChatHistoryProviderOptions());
    
    agentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider = historyProvider;

================================================================================
10. BUILDER PATTERN
================================================================================

AIAgentBuilder:
  Fluent API for building agent pipelines with middleware

  Usage:
    var agent = new AIAgentBuilder(innerAgent)
        .Use((agent) => new LoggingAgent(agent))  // Add logging
        .Use((agent) => new OpenTelemetryAgent(agent))  // Add tracing
        .UseAIContextProviders(contextProviders)  // Add context
        .Build();

Extensions:
  - FunctionInvocationDelegatingAgentBuilderExtensions
  - LoggingAgentBuilderExtensions
  - OpenTelemetryAgentBuilderExtensions
  - ToolApprovalAgentBuilderExtensions

================================================================================
11. COMPLETE CODE EXAMPLE
================================================================================

// 1. Create chat client (e.g., from OpenAI)
var chatClient = new OpenAIClient(apiKey).AsChatClient("gpt-4");

// 2. Create tools/functions
var searchTool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    (string query) => $"Results for '{query}'",
    name: "search",
    description: "Search for information"
);

var calculateTool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    (int a, int b) => a + b,
    name: "add",
    description: "Add two numbers"
);

// 3. Create agent options
var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
{
    Id = "research-agent",
    Name = "Research Agent",
    Description = "Helps research topics",
    ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = new List<AITool> { searchTool, calculateTool },
        ToolMode = ChatToolMode.Auto,
        Temperature = 0.7,
        MaxOutputTokens = 2000
    },
    ChatHistoryProvider = new InMemoryChatHistoryProvider(
        new InMemoryChatHistoryProviderOptions())
};

// 4. Create agent
var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
    chatClient: chatClient,
    options: agentOptions,
    loggerFactory: loggerFactory,
    services: null
);

// 5. Create session for multi-turn conversation
var session = await agent.CreateSessionAsync(
    conversationId: "session-123",
    cancellationToken: cancellationToken
);

// 6. Run agent
var response = await agent.RunAsync(
    message: "Search for AI and calculate 5 + 3",
    session: session,
    options: new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(),
    cancellationToken: cancellationToken
);

Console.WriteLine($"Agent Response: {response.Content}");

// 7. Continue multi-turn conversation
var followUp = await agent.RunAsync(
    message: "What about machine learning?",
    session: session,
    options: new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(),
    cancellationToken: cancellationToken
);

================================================================================
12. KEY NAMESPACE STRUCTURE
================================================================================

Microsoft.Agents.AI
  - ChatClientAgent (Main agent)
  - ChatClientAgentOptions, ChatClientAgentRunOptions
  - AgentSession (For multi-turn)
  - AIAgentBuilder (Middleware pipeline)
  - AIContextProvider (Context enrichment)
  - ChatHistoryProvider (History management)
  - AgentSkillsProvider (Skill registration)
  - Various context providers (TextSearchProvider, TodoProvider, etc.)

Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions
  - AIContextProvider (Base)
  - ChatHistoryProvider (Base)
  - AgentSession, AgentWorkerSession
  - Skill-related abstractions

Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions
  - AITool, AIFunction (Tools/Functions)
  - AIFunctionFactory (Tool creation)
  - AIFunctionDeclaration
  - ChatOptions (Chat configuration)
  - FunctionCallContent, FunctionResultContent
  - Hosted tools (WebSearch, CodeInterpreter, etc.)

Microsoft.Extensions.AI
  - FunctionInvokingChatClient (Auto-invocation)
  - ChatClientBuilder (Builder pattern)
  - Various decorators (Logging, Caching, OpenTelemetry)

================================================================================
13. EXECUTION FLOW DIAGRAM
================================================================================

User Input
  ↓
Agent.RunAsync(message, session, options)
  ↓
AIContextProviders.InvokeAsync (enrich context)
  ↓
ChatHistoryProvider.GetChatHistoryAsync (load history)
  ↓
ChatClient.CompleteAsync with tools list
  ↓
Does model request tool call?
  ├─ YES: FunctionInvokingChatClient catches and invokes
  │   ↓
  │   Find tool in Tools list
  │   ↓
  │   Marshal JSON args to function signature
  │   ↓
  │   Invoke AIFunction locally
  │   ↓
  │   Wrap result in FunctionResultContent
  │   ↓
  │   Loop back to ChatClient with result
  │
  └─ NO: Return final response
      ↓
      FunctionResultContent aggregated
      ↓
      ChatHistoryProvider.AddChatHistoryAsync
      ↓
      AIContextProviders.InvokeAsync (process results)
      ↓
      Return ChatMessage to caller

================================================================================
